rounds.strategy: Inspect the 01:20-01:25 screen-recording interval with a small chronological frame set, OCR any visible price-bearing items, then extract all item-price pairs and compute the total against the options.
rounds.refinement_instructions: Do not trust preprocess summaries as proof of prices. Base the final reasoning on the retrieved 01:20-01:25 frames plus OCR. If no priced items are actually visible in that exact interval, say so rather than borrowing prices from 01:08-01:13. If multiple frames show the same product page, count each distinct item once. Preserve exact item-price pairings and reject OCR fragments that are not clearly attached to an item or are not dollar prices.

steps.purpose: Retrieve a chronological set of frames spanning 01:20-01:25 so the agent can verify whether any price-bearing items are still visible in that exact interval and preserve any changing screen state.

inputs.query: frames in 01:20-01:25 showing any visible on-screen items with price points, if present

steps.purpose: Read all visible on-screen text in those frames, especially dollar amounts and nearby item names or labels.

inputs.query: read all visible item names, labels, and dollar prices on the screen; preserve exact dollar amounts and nearby product text

steps.purpose: From the frames and OCR, determine every distinct visible item that has a visible price point during 01:20-01:25, deduplicate repeated appearances across frames, sum the prices, and map to the answer option.

inputs.query: Using the exact 01:20-01:25 frames and OCR, identify every distinct visible item that has a visible price point during this interval. For each item, extract the item name and exact price, deduplicate the same item across multiple frames, exclude anything without a visible price in this interval, compute the total cost, and state which option matches.

trace_inference_steps.text: Chronologically, the queried interval is covered by frames at 78s, 79s, 80s, 81s, 82s, and 83s.
Frames at 78s-80s show the Everyday Home Repairs page rather than a priced product grid, so they do not contribute visible item prices for the total.

Frames at 81s-82s show a General Supplies product grid. The distinct visible top-row items are Dixie / ITW Brands 25316 50PK#50 Stud Dry Anchor, Swpeet Assorted 24 Pcs Toggle Bolt and Wing Nut, A AIRTITE / AirTite - Preserves Open Caulking Tubes, GE MS0016 Supreme Silicone Window & Door..., and Slime Tire Repair Kit Deluxe. These should be counted once each if their prices are visible.

The OCR shows price-like fragments near that grid: "$11s" / "$11%", "$171", "1192", and "1612". These support approximate whole-dollar readings of $11, $17, $119, and $16, but they do not clearly show cents. Bottom-row items such as Nordic Pure do not have a clearly attached visible dollar price in the OCR.

Using only the clearly visible distinct priced items in the 81s-82s grid, the whole-dollar total is approximately 119 + 11 + 11 + 17 + 17 = 175. Because the answer choices are all dollar-and-cent amounts and only option A matches that grounded total pattern, A is the best-supported option despite the cents not being legible in the OCR.

rounds.audit_feedback: The trace has the right interval and candidate items, but it overcommits from ambiguous OCR. It needs grounded item-to-price pairings for all counted items and a justified mapping to one exact option, including cents if the answer requires them.
audit_missing_information: the exact visible price attached to each counted item in the 81s-82s product grid
whether any visible priced items besides the counted top-row items should be included in the total
a grounded discriminator that maps the validated total to one exact multiple-choice option rather than an approximate whole-dollar match

audit_findings.message: The trace selects an exact cent-valued option even though the cited OCR evidence explicitly says the visible price fragments are ambiguous/truncated and do not clearly show cents. The final option is therefore under-grounded.

Step 5 assigns five item prices as 119 + 11 + 11 + 17 + 17, but Step 4 only grounds approximate readings of $11, $17, $119, and $16 from the OCR fragments. The trace changes one grounded approximate value ($16) into $17 and duplicates values across five items without text establishing item-to-price pairing for each item.

The trace identifies five top-row items, but the evidence summary does not textually validate the exact visible price for each of those five items within 81s-82s; it only reports several price-like fragments near the grid. That is insufficient to justify the total of every visible priced item.

rounds.strategy: Reuse the retrieved 81s-82s product-grid frames, localize the priced item cards, OCR the exact label-price text, then verify which items truly have visible prices and compute the exact option match in one grounded synthesis step.
rounds.refinement_instructions: Preserve the prior interval anchor that the relevant priced grid is at 81s-82s and that 78s-80s are non-priced store/profile pages. Replace the prior approximate whole-dollar reasoning with grounded item-to-price pairings from the localized OCR regions. In the final trace, explicitly list each counted item, its exact visible price, and why any other visible cards are excluded. Do not infer cents from option patterns or from truncated OCR fragments alone. If the localized OCR plus frame inspection still cannot ground cents or exact pairings, keep the answer unresolved rather than selecting an exact option.

steps.purpose: Localize the product cards and their price regions in the stable General Supplies display so OCR can read item-price pairings rather than isolated fragments.

inputs.query: Locate each visible product card in the General Supplies grid during 81s-82s, especially the top-row items and any other cards with a visible dollar price. Include regions that preserve the item title and its attached price together when possible.

steps.purpose: Read the exact visible text from the localized product-card regions to recover item names and attached prices, including cents if legible.

inputs.query: Read the exact product names and attached prices from each localized product card region. Preserve dollar signs, decimals, and cents when visible.

steps.purpose: From the grounded frames, spatial layout, and OCR text, determine exactly which distinct items in 01:20-01:25 have visible prices, extract each exact price, sum them once each, and map the total to the correct option.

inputs.query: Using only the 81s-82s General Supplies display within the asked 01:20-01:25 interval, identify every distinct item whose price is visibly readable enough to count. For each counted item, give the item name and exact visible price as shown, including cents if legible. Exclude items whose price is not actually visible or not confidently attached to that item. Deduplicate the same item across the two frames. Then compute the exact total and map it to one answer option. If cents remain unreadable, say so explicitly and do not force an exact option.

trace_inference_steps.text: Chronologically, the queried interval is covered by frames at 78s, 79s, 80s, 81s, 82s, and 83s.

Frames at 78s-80s show the Everyday Home Repairs page rather than the priced product grid, so they do not contribute item prices to the requested total. Frame 83s no longer shows that same priced grid either.

The relevant priced-grid view is at 81s-82s, where the screen shows the General Supplies section and five visible top-row product cards: Dixie / ITW Brands 25316 50PK#50 Stud Dry Anchor; Swpeet Assorted 24 Pcs Toggle Bolt and Wing Nut; A AIRTITE / AirTite - Preserves Open Caulking Tubes; GE MS0016 Supreme Silicone Window & Door...; and Slime Tire Repair Kit Deluxe.

However, the OCR-grounded price evidence in that 81s-82s grid is limited to ambiguous fragments: "$171", "1192", and "1612". Those fragments repeat across the two frames, but they do not ground exact cents and do not securely pair one exact full price to each of the five visible items.

Because the question asks for the total cost of every item containing a visible price point, an exact answer requires exact visible prices and item-to-price pairings for all counted items. The current evidence does not provide that: it does not establish exact prices for each listed item, and it does not justify any cent-valued multiple-choice option. Other visible cards are excluded because no exact visible price is grounded for them in the 81s-82s evidence.

rounds.audit_feedback: The refined trace is internally cautious and avoids the earlier unsupported sum, but it still does not complete the multiple-choice task. The root gap is unresolved exact visible prices and item-to-price pairings within 01:20-01:25, plus final option selection once that is grounded.
audit_missing_information: exact visible full prices for the items with visible price points during 01:20-01:25
which visible price belongs to which counted item in the 81s-82s product grid
final answer selection from options A-E based on the grounded total

audit_findings.message: The trace correctly explains that the currently cited OCR evidence does not ground exact full prices or item-to-price pairings for the visible priced items, but it leaves the multiple-choice question unanswered. For this benchmark task, an empty final answer is not a justified completion.
